mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and equity discount rates, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive returns occur in more than 55% of triggered instances over the 20-day horizon.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicate synchronized demand expansion, generating positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative median returns over 60 days in more than 60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, marginal increases in industrial production reflect capacity utilization peaks and pricing power, driving short-term positive market momentum.
failure_condition: Signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.01 when INDPRO growth exceeds 2% quarterly.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price index values erode real corporate margins and prompt expectations of tighter monetary policy, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive returns dominate in more than 65% of high-inflation months over the 20-day horizon.

mechanism_narrative: Strong quarterly GDP growth exceeding 2.5% confirms robust economic expansion and corporate earnings growth, producing positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Returns are negative in more than 50% of high-growth quarters over the 60-day horizon.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate signal broad-based labor market tightening, boosting consumer confidence and equity valuations.
failure_condition: Combined signal yields negative returns in more than 55% of observations over the 20-day horizon.

mechanism_narrative: In post-shock recovery phases, a sudden drop in housing starts indicates credit market stress and reduced household leverage capacity, triggering short-term negative equity returns.
failure_condition: Signal fails to predict negative returns when HOUST declines by more than 5% month-over-month.
